title: MULTIMODAL ROBOTIC SYSTEM
what: Bio-inspired 2-DOF multimodal robotic system following blue crab physiology with adaptive flipper morphology for air and water environments
Full-stack architecture spanning soft actuator design, chassis development, electronics/sensor integration, and control software through the systems engineering lifecycle
Research objective: characterize how aerial-to-marine medium transitions shape dynamic gait behavior and control requirements
9 flipper design iterations (air + underwater configurations) and 8 robot CAD iterations across 5 physical prototypes
how: Mechanical: CNC machining + laser cutting for all structural components (brackets, base, electronics board, soft actuators); DFMA-driven design; carbon fiber + Icarex reinforced air flipper; compliant bare soft actuator underwater flipper

Electronics: 4× Dynamixel XW430-T200 servos via U2D2 Power Hub (RS-485); Adafruit ICM-20948 9-axis IMU (I2C); DWE StellarHD camera (USB); dual-rail 3S LiPo power distribution; NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano 8GB compute
Control: 5-node ROS2 Jazzy stack at 400 Hz closed-loop; cascaded PID; telemetry-driven gait engine; Gazebo Harmonic SIL simulation with hybrid real/simulated servo architecture
V&V: Layered program per IEEE 1012, MIL-STD-882E, IEC 61508; V-Model from component verification through system validation; SIL, HIL, and CI/CD regression testing
results: Expected: novel multimodal gait behavior characterization quantifying dynamic response to aerial-to-marine medium transition; resonance shift, damping ratio, and control coupling characterization across both environments

Ongoing: aerial gait behavior testing and characterization; underwater testing and characterization; sensor fusion integration (AprilTag + IMU + encoder state estimation); full closed-loop autonomous gait control
